sections.name: Khravgodon
Tail Tales
sections.text: A Kellid legend holds that the khravgodon once had a beautiful tail covered with bright, thick fur. One night, the khravgodon fell asleep in the open, having danced all night showing off its beautiful tail. When it awoke, ankhravs had chewed all the hair off khravgodon's tail, leaving it with the bald appendage we see today. Khravgodons have been taking revenge on ankhravs ever since.
Few creatures can shrug off an ankhrav's acid and crunch its chitin like a khravgodon.

family.name: Opossum
family.text: Few creatures have survived as long and in as many environments as the opossum.

traits.name: Animal
traits.text: An animal is a creature with a relatively low intelligence. It typically doesn’t have an Intelligence ability modifier over –4, can’t speak languages, and can’t be trained in Intelligence-based skills.

automatic_abilities.effect: The opossum collapses. It is flat-footed and can use actions that require only its mind, but any other action ends the ruse. A successful DC 18 Perception check to Seek or Medicine check to Recall Knowledge is required to determine that the animal is not, in fact, dead.
automatic_abilities.name: Feign Death
automatic_abilities.trigger: The opossum is reduced below 70 HP

automatic_abilities.effect: The opossum Strikes the triggering creature.
automatic_abilities.name: Revived Retaliation
automatic_abilities.trigger: The opossum is attacked or disturbed by a creature within reach while Feigning Death

modifiers.name: +2 circumstance to all saves vs. disease

traits.name: Deadly
traits.text: On a critical hit, the weapon adds a weapon damage die of the listed size. Roll this after doubling the weapon's damage. This increases to two dice if the weapon has a _greater striking rune_ and three dice if the weapon has a _major striking rune_. For instance, a rapier with a _greater striking rune_ deals 2d8 extra piercing damage on a critical hit. An ability that changes the size of the weapon's normal damage dice doesn't change the size of its deadly die.

traits.name: Agile
traits.text: The multiple attack penalty you take with this weapon on the second attack on your turn is –4 instead of –5, and –8 instead of –10 on the third and subsequent attacks in the turn.

traits.name: Reach
traits.text: Natural attacks with this trait can be used to attack creatures up to the listed distance away instead of only adjacent creatures. Weapons with this trait are long and can be used to attack creatures up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent creatures. For creatures that already have reach with the limb or limbs that wield the weapon, the weapon increases their reach by 5 feet.

ability.effect: The khravgodon bites the creature, dealing 2d12+9 piercing damage (DC 28 basic Fortitude save) that ignores the first 5 of the target's Hardness or resistance to physical damage. On a failed save, the target also takes a –2 circumstance penalty to AC for 1 round.
ability.name: Crush Chitin
ability.requirement: The khravgodon has a creature grabbed or restrained

ability.name: Grasping Tail
ability.text: A khravgodon can drag a Large or smaller creature it has grabbed with its tail along with it when it Strides.

universal_monster_ability.name: Low-Light Vision
universal_monster_ability.text: The monster can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so it ignores the concealed condition due to dim light.

universal_monster_ability.name: Scent
universal_monster_ability.text:
Scent involves sensing creatures or objects by smell, and is usually a vague sense. The range is listed in the ability, and it functions only if the creature or object being detected emits an aroma (for instance, incorporeal creatures usually do not exude an aroma).  
  
 If a creature emits a heavy aroma or is upwind, the GM can double or even triple the range of scent abilities used to detect that creature, and the GM can reduce the range if a creature is downwind.
